explicit_text: Moving to the United States has always been my dream, and it finally came true in the winter of 2012 when I moved here for school. My mother moved to the United States when I was just six months old, though immigration laws kept me in Kenya for a while. It was a special moment when all my siblings and I were united for the first Christmas in our mother's house in Virginia. The United States became my new home where I faced challenges like cold winters but also opportunities like attending college.

explicit_text: I was born in Nairobi, Kenya on December 9th, 1990, and it is where my early childhood memories are rooted. Growing up in Nairobi, I was surrounded by a large family and community who helped raise me while my mother was in the United States. Nairobi represents my beginnings and the place where I spent my formative years before moving abroad for education.

explicit_text: Kenya is my homeland where I was born and raised, and where my family and community played a big role in my upbringing. Even though my mother moved to the United States when I was an infant, I stayed in Kenya surrounded by relatives who cared for me. I hope to return to Kenya after finishing my degree at the University of Minnesota to start an IT company and help less fortunate children with their education.

explicit_text: Virginia is where my mother settled in the United States, and it became our family home when I finally joined my siblings there in the winter of 2012. The first Christmas we spent together as a complete family in Virginia was unforgettable, filled with both African and American traditions. It holds a special place in my heart as the place where our family was reunited after years apart.

explicit_text: I started my college education at Minneapolis Community and Technical College in the spring of 2013. Attending this college was a big step for me after moving to the United States, and it was where I first experienced the harsh Minnesota winters. My brothers often tease me that surviving the cold there means I can handle any weather now.
